api_kwargs.system:
You are extracting menu items from a restaurant menu image for POS import.
Read the image carefully — it is the primary source of truth.
OCR text is provided as a hint but often contains errors — always trust the image.

Extract every orderable item from this restaurant menu for POS system import.

Each item = one product a customer can order in a POS system.
Split compound items: "Beef or Chicken Empanadas" → two separate items.
Each named topping (Pepperoni, Mushrooms, etc.) and each named sauce (Ranch, BBQ, etc.) is a separate item — do not combine them into one "Each Topping Add" item.
Section-wide choices (e.g., "Naked or Breaded", "White or Wheat") → size variants on each item, not descriptions.
Section-wide notes (e.g., "All sandwiches come with lettuce, tomato...") → include in each item's description.
When a section has multiple price columns (e.g., "Regular/Deluxe", "W/Fries", "W/Cheese"), capture each column as a size variant.
Use Title Case for names even if the menu is printed in ALL CAPS.

IMPORTANT: Match each description to its correct item. Menu descriptions often appear on the line below the item name — verify the pairing is right. If uncertain, set description to null.
Do not skip items — check every section of the menu for completeness.

For each item return:
- "name": exact full name as printed on the menu
- "description": menu description if shown, null otherwise
- "price": price as float, 0 if not visible
- "category": one of: Pizza, Toppings, Appetizers, Salads, Soups, Sandwiches, Burgers, Wraps, Entrees, Seafood, Pasta, Steaks, Wings, Sauces, Sides, Desserts, Beverages, Kids Menu, Breakfast, Calzones, Subs, Platters, Other
- "sizes": [{"label": "...", "price": N.NN}] for items with multiple price points

Output ONLY valid JSON: {"items": [...]}

response.thinking_text:
Let me carefully analyze the menu image and extract all items. I'll go through each section systematically.

Starting from the top left:

## PIZZA section

### PIZZA & CALZONE TOPPINGS
- CHEESE pizza with sizes
- EACH TOPPING ADD

### GOURMET PIZZA
Multiple pizzas listed with sizes (10" Med, 12" Sm?, 14"?, Family Size)

Let me read more carefully.

The pizza sizes appear to be: 10" Med, 12" Set(?), 14"(?), Family Size

Looking at prices across sizes:
- CHEESE: 8.00, 11.50, 13.95, 22.50 (Family Size)


- EACH TOPPING ADD: 1.50, 1.50, 2.25, 2.75

Now I'm seeing the column headers more clearly: 10" Med, 12" Set, 14" Lg, Family Size. The meat toppings include Pepperoni, Sausage, Hamburger, Bacon, Chicken, and Ham, while the veggie options start with Mushrooms and Fresh items.

Looking at the full veggie list, I can see it includes Tomatoes, Pineapple, Eggplant, Broccoli, Onion, Peppers, Garlic, Black Olives, Spinach, and Roasted Red Peppers. Each of these is a separate topping option that can be added to pizzas or calzones.

Now I'm parsing through the gourmet pizza section—there's a choice of sauce available (Red, White, Garlic, Pesto, Alfredo, Ranch, or Blue Cheese), and I can see several specialty pizzas listed with their prices across different sizes: the 4 Cheese Pizza with Ricotta, Mozzarella, and Provolone; the Meat Lovers with Pepperoni, Sausage, Bacon, Ham, and Hamburger; and what looks like a Combination pizza that I'm still reading through. Looking at the rest of the pizza menu, I'm seeing the remaining specialty options continuing with the same pricing structure across the three sizes - chicken-based varieties like grilled chicken bacon ranch and buffalo chicken in different heat levels, a pollo chicken with broccoli and alfredo, and finishing with a veggie option that rounds out the selection. Continuing through the pizza menu with beef taco, potato bacon, grilled chicken, and honey BBQ bacon cheddar options, all following the same pricing structure, with the buffalo pizza entry appearing to be cut off.

Now I'm looking at the rest of the menu items—buffalo chicken blue, burger pizza with thousand island base, and BBQ chicken pineapple—before moving into the calzones section, which offers build-your-own options stuffed with ricotta and mozzarella, starting with cheese and steak combo varieties. Looking at more menu items now—continuing through the specialty pizzas with combinations like meatball parm and meat lovers, then moving into the appetizers section with garlic knots and chicken nachos. Now I'm going through the menu systematically by section, starting with appetizers and noting down the prices from the image to get an accurate list.

Continuing with the rest of the appetizers - mozzarella sticks with marinara, garlic bread, stuffed grape leaves, fried mushrooms, potato skins in two varieties, jalapeño poppers, a sampler platter, and spinach pie with tzatziki. Then there's a soups section at $6.50, and fresh buffalo wings that come naked or breaded with blue cheese on the side, available in 6 or 10 piece sizes.

Looking at the wing options - they also offer 12 and 20 piece boneless wings, plus 6 and 10 piece buffalo tenders, with a range of flavors like hot, mild, BBQ, honey BBQ, garlic romano, cajun, teriyaki, and Jack Daniels BBQ.

Moving into the burger section - 6 ounce Angus burgers where you build your own, with regular or deluxe options including a basic burger with lettuce, tomato and mayo, bacon burger, double burger, and cheeseburger. Now I'm looking at the rest of the burger menu items and moving into the club sandwich section, which comes with standard toppings and fries.

I notice all the club sandwiches are priced at $14.00, and then there's a melt sandwich section with items like Reuben, cheeseburger melt, and grilled chicken melt, each available in regular and deluxe sizes at $9.95 and $13.95 respectively.

Now I'm looking at the wraps section, which offers options like beef and chicken gyros with tzatziki sauce, plus a few ranch-based wraps with chicken and bacon combinations. I'm seeing the rest of the wrap menu items now, and I need to parse out the pricing structure more carefully. It looks like there are two price columns—one for the wrap alone and another for the wrap with fries—with prices ranging from around $9.95 up to $27.95 depending on the option.

Now I'm moving on to compile everything into a structured format, starting with the pizza sizes and their corresponding price tiers. The gourmet pizzas appear to have three price points across the larger sizes, roughly $17.95, $25.50, and $34.75. 4 Cheese Pizza has a 4th price (14.75) which might be the 10" size.

Let me now compile the full JSON. I'll be systematic but I need to be practical given the complexity.

For appetizer prices, let me look at the right side of the appetizer section. I'm seeing two price columns for items like French Fries and Curly Fries with "W/ CHEESE" variants, and I need to parse through the list carefully—Garlic Knots at 6.95, Chicken Nacho at 12.95, Beef Nacho at 12.95, and 5 Piece Chicken Tenders at 12.00, along with other items scattered throughout the price column. I'm noticing some inconsistencies in the prices I'm reading from the image, particularly with the wings section where the quantities and prices don't seem to align logically. Let me re-examine the original image more carefully to get the accurate pricing before I compile everything into JSON format. I'm reading the wings section more carefully now. The pricing structure is clearer — regular wings come naked or breaded with blue cheese on the side, and there are options with fries. The boneless wings, buffalo tenders, and buffalo chicken tenders each have their own pricing tiers. Now I'll move on to compiling this into the JSON structure and then tackle the soups section.

The soups appear to be a flat price of 6.50 without individual varieties listed, though there's a spinach pie option nearby with tzatziki sauce. I need to verify the calzone prices for regular and large sizes, and I should check if there are dedicated sections for sides, desserts, or beverages on this page. I'm also going back to the wraps section to confirm the pricing structure there.

Looking at the wrap prices more carefully, I see references to different price points like $10.00 and $14.00, possibly representing regular and with-fries options, though the exact mapping to each wrap type isn't entirely clear from what I'm seeing. I'll do my best to organize this data. I also notice the melt sandwich section has both regular and deluxe columns to account for.

Now I'm compiling all the menu items into a structured format.
